item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angelina Nikolau, 33.

item.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers in custody after apparently getting engaged at the top
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Trump claims he isn't involved in his personal finances after crypto disclosure
item.description: President Trump claimed Wednesday he is not involved in his personal financial dealings. Disclosure forms released Tuesday revealed he made more than a billion dollars in cryptocurrency ventures last year. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: CBS News journalists unpack rumored Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Rumors are swirling about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tying the knot at Madison Square Garden. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Gabrielle Ake, self-described Swifties, join "The Takeout" with the latest.

item.title: Jaylen Brown reportedly traded from Celtics to 76ers
item.description: Jaylen Brown is done with the Boston Celtics. The Celtics have traded Brown to Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: Sweeping changes to federal student loans take effect
item.description: Significant changes to federal student loan policies took effect on Wednesday. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger joins "The Takeout" to explain what borrowers need to know.

item.title: H. R. McMaster says Iran is "getting everything that they wanted" in U.S. talks
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday touted progress in negotiations between the U.S. and Iran. Former Trump national security adviser H. R. McMaster joins with analysis.

item.title: Takeaways from Colorado democratic socialist Melat Kiros' House primary win
item.description: Democratic socialists scored another primary victory on Tuesday, with challenger Melat Kiros defeating longtime Colorado Rep. Diana DeGette. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto joins with analysis.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: Trump filing reveals he made $2.2 billion in 2025, unprecedented in presidential history
item.description: President Trump and his businesses made more than $2 billion in 2025, according to a new financial disclosure. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports. Then, New York Times reporter Andrea Fuller joins to discuss further.

item.title: More than 1,000 cases under review after Broadview Six misconduct revelations
item.description: More than 1,000 grand jury presentations are under review after charges were dismissed in the "Broadview Six" case​ due to grand jury abuses and prosecutorial misconduct, Chicago's top federal prosecutor said.

item.title: Supreme Court ruling on transgender athletes ban doesn't change NCAA's policy, Baker says
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told CBS News' Ed O'Keefe that the Supreme Court's decision to uphold state laws banning transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women's sports does not change the college organization's transgender athletes policy. "After I got this job ... we adopted and complied with the standard that was put forth by the Trump administration. I think what happens at the state level is a different question," Baker said in an interview airing Sunday on "Face the Nation."

item.title: Trump criticizes Supreme Court, Spain, NATO in North Dakota comments
item.description: President Donald Trump delivered remarks in North Dakota Wednesday for the opening of the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library. The trip marked his first flight on the new Air Force One, a gift from the Qatari government to the U.S. last year. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: About 2,000 U.S. troops supporting relief efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Almost 60,000 buildings may have been damaged or destroyed in Venezuela after two powerful earthquakes last week, according to a NASA satellite assessment.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Couples are spending thousands more on weddings. Here's why.
item.description: Tariffs, inflation and changing consumer habits are reshaping how much Americans spend to tie the knot.

item.title: One NYC neighborhood has dozens of adult daycares. Now the feds have questions.
item.description: Investigations are underway into possible fraudulent activity at some of these kinds of facilities across New York, CBS News has learned.

item.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: Harvard professor known for alien theories to lead White House UFO council
item.description: Avi Loeb, a Harvard astronomer who has made headlines with his theories about alien encounters, will lead a White House scientific advisory council on UFOs.

item.title: Why U.S. initially restricted Anthropic's advanced AI models
item.description: The Trump administration has lifted export restrictions on Anthropic's most advanced artificial intelligence models, Fable and Mythos, after weeks of negotiations over national security concerns. CNET Editor-in-Chief David Katzmaier joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump responds to criticism of his crypto earnings: "Everybody's profiting"
item.description: President Trump responded to critics on Wednesday who say he's profiting off the presidency after a new financial disclosure showed he made over $1 billion on cryptocurrency ventures in 2025. Victor Reklaitis, Washington correspondent for MarketWatch, joins with analysis.

item.title: House lawmakers start July 4th recess early after some GOP reps block bill merger
item.description: House lawmakers began their July 4th recess early on Tuesday after some GOP members blocked Speaker Mike Johnson from merging the SAVE America Act with the annual defense policy bill, known as the National Defense Authorization Act. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: U.S. says it won't extend key trade deal with Canada and Mexico
item.description: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement will remain in effect until it expires in 2036, unless the countries strike another deal to extend it.

item.title: DOJ investigating Sen. Ruben Gallego's use of campaign funds, sources say
item.description: The probe focuses on Gallego's use of campaign funds for family trips, a source told CBS News. He has denied wrongdoing.

item.title: What is the "buy, borrow, die" tax strategy Gavin Newsom wants to ban?
item.description: As a proposed billionaire tax in California moves forward, Gov. Newsom says other approaches are needed, including closing a tax loophole used by the ultra-rich.

item.title: South Carolina woman on her values, story and Southern accent as America celebrates 250 years
item.description: For America's 250th anniversary, CBS News spoke to Karen Norris Newsome, a 74-year-old woman from Darlington County, South Carolina, about her childhood, her values and her quintessential Southern accent.

item.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin. lifted restrictions on powerful Claude AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence giant Anthropic says the federal government lifted a set of restrictions on its powerful Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, resolving a weekslong dispute.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" on July 4
item.description: "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil and "Entertainment Tonight" co-host Nischelle Turner will host "The Great American Block Party 250," a primetime special on July 4 celebrating America's 250th birthday from Washington, D.C.
